description: All authored Freddy Fractions dialogue. Keys map 1:1 to MP3 filenames in /public/audio/. Lines may contain {{NAME}} which is split at build time so the kid's name MP3 is stitched at runtime (see PRD §3.11). Most lines use 'kid' as a generic address term — Freddy calls everyone 'kid'. The {{NAME}} slot is reserved for the single recognition beat right after the kid types their name (`onboarding_response`); everywhere else, 'kid' keeps the rhythm and avoids name-stitch breaks.

voice.notes: Super Mario meets Jersey Shore — Italian-American chef energy, warm, lovable, expressive. Selected from ElevenLabs Voice Library by Jason. Voice must be added to the account's voice library before the API can call it (Voice Library → Add to my voices).

lines.onboarding_greeting: Heyyy, welcome to SuperSlice! I'm Freddy Fractions. What's your name, kid?
lines.onboarding_response: {{NAME}}, beautiful name. Alright, lemme show ya how this works.
lines.explore_intro_2: Slicer and glove are right down there.
lines.explore_intro_4: Deliveries go in the box. Have a play around.
lines.explore_cue: Just tap me on the shoulder when you're done messin' around.
lines.aha_setup: Hey kid, want to see something cool? I just pulled this fresh pizza outta the oven — it's already cut in half. Try slicing one of those halves once more, just for me.
lines.aha_wrong_slice: Hey, nice cut! But try slicing one of the halves we already made, alright?
lines.aha_stuck: C'mon, kid — try tapping the slicer on one of those halves.
lines.aha_compare_prompt: Beautiful! Now drag those two quarters right next to a half. Tell me what you notice.
lines.aha_not_equal: Hmm, those ain't quite the same yet. Try moving them right up against each other.
lines.aha_stuck_compare: Slide the quarters right up against the half, kid. You'll see it.
lines.aha_reveal: Whoa, kid! Look at that — one half is the SAME as two quarters! You just made fraction equivalence! Bellissimo!
lines.lesson_win: Bellissimo, kid! You put it back together — just like a real pizza chef!
lines.lesson_intro: Alright kid, two hungry friends just ordered. One pizza, gotta split it fair. Can you slice it right down the middle so each friend gets the SAME amount?
lines.lesson_react_halves: Boom — two equal halves! Each friend gets one half. Now, two MORE friends showed up. Four hungry kids, same pizza. Can you cut each half one more time?
lines.lesson_stuck_halves: Try the slicer on the pizza, kid. Cut it right down the middle.
lines.lesson_react_quarters: Four equal quarters! Each friend gets one piece. But here's where it gets cool...
lines.lesson_wrong_eighths: Hey, those pieces are getting tiny! Try slicing one of those bigger halves instead.
lines.lesson_stuck_quarters: Tap the slicer on one of those halves, kid. Cut it in half again.
lines.lesson_compare_prompt: Now drag two of those quarters right up next to one half. Tell me what you notice.
lines.lesson_not_equal: Get them right up against each other, kid. Real close.
lines.lesson_stuck_compare: Grab TWO quarter pieces and slide them right next to that half. You'll see it.
lines.lesson_reveal: Whoa, {{NAME}}! Look at that — one half is the EXACT same size as two quarters. That's fraction equivalence. One-half equals two-quarters! Bellissimo!
lines.lesson_end: You did it, kid! You just proved that one-half equals two-quarters. That's real math. Bellissimo!
lines.react_eighths: Eighths! Look at all those slices.
lines.lesson_intro_v2: Alright, here's the deal. Carlo wants a pizza, and his buddy Marco does too. One pizza, two of 'em. How do we split it fair?
lines.lesson_stuck_halves_v2: Grab the cutter and slice the pizza right down the middle. Two equal pieces.
lines.lesson_react_halves_v2: That's it. Two equal halves. Each piece is one of two — we call that one-half. Now watch this — cut just ONE of those halves into two pieces. Trust me.
lines.lesson_stuck_mixed_v2: Pick just one half — leave the other one alone — and slice it in two.
lines.lesson_react_mixed_v2: Now we've got one half AND two quarters. Same pizza, just sliced different. Here's the thing — push those two quarters together right next to the half. Look close.
lines.lesson_react_mixed_alt_v2: Even cleaner — you made four quarters. Same idea — pick TWO of those quarters and slide 'em together. See how they line up next to the other two.
lines.lesson_wrong_eighths_v2: Whoa, those pieces are getting tiny. Try the cutter on a bigger piece — a half or a quarter.
lines.lesson_stuck_compare_v2: Slide two quarters right up close together. Get 'em side by side.
lines.lesson_reveal_v2: See it, {{NAME}}? Two quarters lined up are exactly the same size as one half. Two-of-four equals one-of-two. That's called equivalent fractions — different names, same amount of pizza.
lines.lesson_win_v2: Beautiful work, {{NAME}}. Fractions can look different but be equal — that's a real chef's secret. Carlo and Marco are gonna eat good tonight.
lines.v3_beat_1_distribute_4: These two boxes are for Maya and Theo, {{NAME}}. See if you can divide these pizzas evenly between them.
lines.v3_beat_3_math_statement: 4 pizzas divided by 2 people equals 2 each.
lines.v3_beat_4_distribute_5: But how about 5 pizzas divided by 2 people? Try giving each person the same amount.
lines.v3_beat_6_mc_cut_or_dog: What should we do with the extra pizza?
lines.v3_beat_7_knife: Great idea, let me get a knife.
lines.v3_beat_8_slice_leftover: Tap the leftover pizza to slice it in half.
lines.v3_beat_10_distribute_halves: Now give each person one of those halves.
lines.v3_beat_12_mc_count: How many pizzas would you say each person got?
lines.v3_beat_13_right: Right. Two and a half pizzas.
lines.v3_beat_14_intro_notation: Now let's write down what each person got.
lines.v3_beat_15_two_numeral: We can just write the number 2 for the whole pizzas. No problem.
lines.v3_beat_18_yn_know_one_half: Do you know how to write the fraction for one half?
lines.v3_beat_19_type_one_half: Go for it. What's one half written as a fraction?
lines.v3_beat_21_so_each_person: So each person has two and a half pizzas.
lines.v3_beat_21_5_stop_or_continue: You just wrote your first fraction! Want to keep going to harder ones like quarters, or stop here for today?
lines.v3_scene_4_intro: Nonna and Nico are joining Maya and Theo — that's four boxes for four hungry friends.
lines.v3_beat_22_now_5_and_4: Now we've got 5 pizzas and 4 people. Try giving each person the same amount.
lines.v3_beat_24_5_distributed: Each friend has 1 pizza, with 1 left over. Tap the leftover to slice it in half.
lines.v3_beat_25_slice_again: Now slice each half — that gives us 4 quarters.
lines.v3_beat_27_distribute_quarters: Now give each person one of those quarters.
lines.v3_beat_28_each_got_extra: Each person got a whole pizza, plus a little extra.
lines.v3_beat_29_mc_quarter_name: This time the extra part is half of a half. Do you know what that's called?
lines.v3_beat_31_mc_count_quarters: How many quarter pizzas are there in a full pizza?
lines.v3_beat_33_type_one_fourth: So if a quarter is 1 out of 4 pieces — guess how we write the fraction for a quarter?
lines.v3_beat_35_quarter_notation: Since a quarter is 1 out of 4 pieces, the fraction for a quarter is one fourth.
lines.v3_beat_36_mc_continue_yn: Want to continue learning about fractions?
